2605.03808.title: Agentic-imodels: Evolving agentic interpretability tools via autoresearch
2605.03808.paper_summary: Agentic data science (ADS) systems are rapidly improving their capability to autonomously analyze, fit, and interpret data, potentially moving towards a future where agents conduct the vast majority of data-science work. However, current ADS systems use statistical tools designed to be interpretable by humans, rather than interpretable by agents. To address this, we introduce Agentic-imodels, an agentic autoresearch loop that evolves data-science tools designed to be interpretable by agents. Specifically, it develops a library of scikit-learn-compatible regressors for tabular data that are optimized for both predictive performance and a novel LLM-based interpretability metric. The metric measures a suite of LLM-graded tests that probe whether a fitted model's string representation is "simulatable" by an LLM, i.e. whether the LLM can answer questions about the model's behavior by reading its string output alone. We find that the evolved models jointly improve predictive performance and agent-facing interpretability, generalizing to new datasets and new interpretability tests. Furthermore, these evolved models improve downstream end-to-end ADS, increasing performance for Copilot CLI, Claude Code, and Codex on the BLADE benchmark by up to 73%
2605.03808.paper_summary_zh: 代理數據科學（ADS）系統正在迅速提升其自主分析、擬合和解釋數據的能力，潛在地朝著一個未來發展，在這個未來中，代理將進行絕大多數的數據科學工作。<lb/>然而，目前的ADS系統使用的是設計為人類可解釋的統計工具，而不是設計為代理可解釋的工具。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們引入了Agentic-imodels，一個代理自我研究循環，旨在發展設計為代理可解釋的數據科學工具。<lb/>具體來說，它開發了一個與scikit-learn兼容的回歸器庫，專為表格數據優化，旨在提升預測性能和一種新穎的基於LLM的可解釋性指標。<lb/>該指標測量一系列LLM評分的測試，探查擬合模型的字符串表示是否能被LLM“模擬”，即LLM是否能僅通過閱讀其字符串輸出來回答有關模型行為的問題。<lb/>我們發現，這些進化的模型共同提高了預測性能和面向代理的可解釋性，並能夠推廣到新的數據集和新的可解釋性測試。<lb/>此外，這些進化的模型改善了下游的端到端ADS，對於Copilot CLI、Claude Code和Codex在BLADE基準測試中的性能提升了多達73%。
2605.03808.author: Chandan Singh et.al.
2605.03808.authors: Chandan Singh, Yan Shuo Tan, Weijia Xu, Zelalem Gero, Weiwei Yang, Michel Galley, Jianfeng Gao
